personal.bio: AI/ML engineer focused on moving LLMs from prototype to production. I architect the multi-agent systems, RAG pipelines, and full-stack applications behind ngedu.ai — a live platform delivering the Australian Curriculum (Year 0–12) — and have brought generative AI into enterprise CRM. Grounded in machine learning, deep learning, NLP, and computer vision, with a current focus on generative AI, agentic systems, and retrieval-augmented generation.

experience.description: Architect and engineering lead for ngedu.ai, a live platform delivering the Australian Curriculum from Year 0 to Year 12. Split teaching into a multi-agent system — AI tutor, classroom agent, daily coach, and parent advisor — so each job is owned by a dedicated agent instead of one overloaded prompt. Built real-time voice tutoring on AWS Nova Sonic streaming with barge-in, so students can interrupt mid-sentence, backed by Amazon Polly TTS. Shipped the full product — React/TypeScript/Tailwind, Supabase (Postgres/pgvector), 70+ serverless edge functions, Stripe subscription billing, and role-based portals — and own production end to end: AWS (Bedrock, S3, Lambda), performance budgets, SEO/GEO, and E2E/load testing.

Engineered features across the enterprise CRM platforms SugarCRM and SuiteCRM. Built a LangGraph-based multi-agent assistant that brings generative AI into day-to-day CRM workflows, with a focus on scalable backend development and clean architecture across the full software development lifecycle.

cloud_data: AWS (Bedrock, Titan, Nova Sonic, Polly, S3, Lambda)

projects.title: NextGen Schooling — ngedu.ai
projects.description: A live AI learning platform delivering the Australian Curriculum (Year 0–12), built on coordinated LLM agents and real-time voice tutoring.

caseStudy.problem: Homeschooling families following the Australian Curriculum have no single system that turns a national syllabus into daily, personalized instruction. Parents stitch together worksheets and videos with no adaptivity — and no reliable read on whether a child is actually on track.
caseStudy.approach: Architected a platform where distinct LLM agents each own a teaching job — an AI tutor for concepts and Q&A, a classroom agent for lesson delivery, a daily coach for pacing, and a parent advisor for progress — instead of overloading a single prompt. Real-time voice tutoring runs on AWS Nova Sonic streaming with barge-in, so a student can interrupt mid-sentence, backed by Amazon Polly TTS. Curriculum content is generated by a syllabus-grounded RAG pipeline (see the RAG Engine case study). Front-end in React/TypeScript/Tailwind; data on Supabase (Postgres + pgvector); 70+ serverless edge functions; Stripe subscription billing; and role-based portals for students, parents, facilitators, and admins.
caseStudy.role: Sole architect and engineering lead. Designed the agent topology, the voice pipeline, the data model, and the billing and portal systems — and own production concerns end to end: AWS (Bedrock, S3, Lambda), performance budgets, SEO/GEO, and E2E + load testing.
caseStudy.impact: Took the product from zero to a live, paid platform covering the full Year 0–12 curriculum, with four coordinated agents and real-time voice running in production.

projects.title: NextGen RAG Engine — ngedu.ai Backend
projects.description: The FastAPI retrieval-and-generation service behind ngedu.ai — turns official syllabus documents into on-grade, on-syllabus readings, quizzes, and slides.

caseStudy.problem: Ask an LLM to "generate a Year 7 science reading" and it hallucinates, drifts off-syllabus, and repeats content across lessons. For a curriculum product, off-syllabus or grade-inappropriate output is a correctness bug — not a cosmetic one.
caseStudy.approach: A FastAPI service that ingests syllabus documents (PDF/DOCX), embeds them with Amazon Titan Text Embeddings V2 into Supabase pgvector, and generates content with Claude 3.5 Sonnet on AWS Bedrock. Retrieval quality is engineered deliberately: hybrid search (BM25 sparse + dense vectors) with reranking and query expansion to survive syllabus phrasing mismatches; a semantic query cache to cut latency and Bedrock cost on repeated generations; and grade-aware validation plus deduplication so output stays on-grade and non-repetitive. Containerized with Docker and deployed on AWS App Runner.
caseStudy.role: Designed and built the pipeline end to end — ingestion, embedding, hybrid retrieval and rerank, semantic caching, grade-aware validation, and deployment.
caseStudy.impact: Turned raw syllabus documents into a grounded generation service that keeps content on-syllabus and on-grade, running live behind ngedu.ai.

projects.title: DocChat-MultiAgent
projects.description: A self-checking document Q&A system where a dedicated verification agent fact-checks each answer against the source before it's returned — the hallucination-control pattern I later applied in production.

caseStudy.problem: Naive RAG chatbots confidently answer from weak or irrelevant retrievals. There's no internal check that the answer is actually grounded in the retrieved source — so wrong-but-fluent responses ship straight to the user.
caseStudy.approach: A multi-agent RAG system orchestrated with LangGraph, where separate agents handle retrieval, answer drafting, and verification. The verifier fact-checks the draft against the retrieved context before anything is returned, rather than trusting a single generation. Documents are parsed with Docling, generation runs on Google Gemini, and the whole thing is wrapped in a Gradio UI.
caseStudy.role: Solo build — designed the agent graph and the verification loop end to end.
caseStudy.impact: A working demonstration of grounded, self-checking document Q&A. The same verification-before-answer pattern informs the grounding approach used in the production RAG engine above.
